verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase subunit beta) is canonically a mitochondrial inner-membrane complex subunit facing the matrix, part of the F1 catalytic head of ATP synthase. However, it is well-documented as an ecto-ATP synthase on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in multiple cell types, particularly cancer cells, hepatocytes, endothelial cells, and immune cells. This cell-surface pool participates in extracellular ATP synthesis and serves as a receptor for angiostatin, apoA-I, and other ligands. Surface expression is context-dependent (cell-type and/or oncogenic/metabolic state), qualifying this as contextual/cell_state_induced rather than constitutive.
